name: empty_arch_satisfies_inv
reason: An architecture with zero layers should not satisfy inv, as it represents no valid page table structure

name: entry_base_zero_neq_base
code:
proof fn phi_2_entry_base_zero_neq_base(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.entry_base(layer, base, 0) != base,
{
}
reason: The base address of the first entry (idx=0) should equal the directory base; inequality would mean a gap or offset error

name: update_changes_layer
code:
proof fn phi_3_update_changes_layer(d: Directory, n: nat, e: NodeEntry)
    requires
        d.inv(),
        n < d.num_entries(),
    ensures
        d.update(n, e).layer != d.layer,
{
}
reason: Updating a single entry should preserve the directory's layer; a change would corrupt the tree structure

name: degenerate_arch_satisfies_inv
reason: An architecture with entry_size=1 byte and a single entry per layer is nonsensical for page tables and should be rejected by inv

name: all_invalid_implies_nonempty_interp
code:
proof fn phi_5_all_invalid_implies_nonempty_interp(d: Directory)
    requires
        d.inv(),
        forall|i: nat| i < d.entries.len() ==> d.entries[i as int] is Invalid,
    ensures
        exists|k: nat| d.interp().contains_key(k),
{
}
reason: A directory whose entries are all Invalid should have an empty interpretation; a non-empty map would mean phantom mappings
